title: artemisia vulgaris var. ludoviciana
intro: Artemisia vulgaris var. ludoviciana, also known as Western Mugwort, is a variant of the common wormwood plant, a perennial herb native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihelmintic properties. The health benefits of Western Mugwort include relieving digestive issues, reducing menstrual cramps, and promoting relaxation. Western Mugwort can be consumed as a tea, added to herbal infusions, or used in tinctures and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
